data.body:
# Epic 3156 — Project-supplied review criteria (unified cross-gate registry): completed end-to-end

## Outcome
Epic 3156 CLOSED (signed plan-review + completion-verifier attestations). All 6 child stories
implemented, tested, closed, and merged to main via PRs:
- ef7e (#103) — open the closed vocabulary: .rebar/criteria_routing.json overlay +
  effective_criteria/effective_routing + content-hash lru_cache isolation + CI parity gate + ADR 0015.
  Fan-out is RUNNER-SIDE (ProductionBatchRunner), NOT a workflow-schema change (the v3 batch schema
  is immutable + the gate self-validates — the original extra_criteria approach broke it).
- 55b8 (#105) — per-criterion eval runner + calibration view: eval_solver.run_case criterion arm
  (pass1_chunk over inline text, no case_store); eval.calibrate_criterion (recall/false-accept/
  Cohen's kappa/agreement/N-run stability); `rebar criteria eval <id>` CLI.
- 7f0d (#106) — data-driven DET-invariant consumer + per-criterion fail_mode, exposed to plan-review:
  code_review detectors.py generalized (detector selector + fail_mode in criteria_routing.json;
  run_security_detectors -> run_detectors alias); exec:DET descriptor branch (skips get_prompt);
  DET excluded from LLM batch by reading exec DIRECTLY (exec_tier has no DET arm — it remaps DET->
  1-TURN, a bug the review caught); plan_review/det_invariants.py two-phase run_det_floor
  (file_impact-scoped). ADR 0016.
- 08af (#107) — overlay-aware registry_version(repo_root) (overlay-absent == packaged, zero churn) +
  stale-regver claim-gate check + built-in disable (`disabled: true`) + disabled_builtins manifest line.
- 5065 (#108) — CAPSTONE: shared rebar.llm.criteria layer both gates delegate to (model.py
  threshold_for(gate=) reconciling default_posture vs blocking_enabled; overlay.py gate-parameterized
  core). code-review gained effective_criteria/overlay support. ADR 0017 + CLAUDE.md how-to.
- 6e31 (#109) — editor POST /criterion/preview (LLM+DET dispatch, async job+poll spike-gate) +
  routing-fields authoring UI + write_criterion_overlay (atomic validate-then-rollback).

## How it went (process notes)
- b744 (code-review capability) LANDED mid-effort, removing the blocker I had flagged; user then chose
  "do all b744-independent work", which became the FULL scope once b744 merged.
- Gate discipline enforced every story: plan-review to PASS -> claim -> implement -> completion-verifier
  to close. Plan-review took many rounds (7f0d = 8) and caught real bugs (extra_criteria vs immutable
  v3 schema; exec_tier DET remap; case_store inapplicability). Noise tax: recurring "decompose" (G5) on
  coherent stories; doc/boundary nitpicks escalated to blocking; non-monotonic convergence.
- Integration bug at the finish: 5065's refactor moved _detector_matches to criteria.model; a
  branch-update merge dropped the back-compat re-export -> det_invariants ImportError -> 5065 CI red.
  Fixed by re-exporting from plan_review.registry. Final macOS CI failure was FLAKY perf tests
  (test_graph_perf.py absolute wall-clock limits) — passed on re-run.
- The epic completion verifier reads the COMMITTED tree, so the epic could only close after ALL 6 PRs
  merged to main (a local uncommitted overlay did NOT satisfy it). Also hit reconciler-truncation of
  the large epic description causing material-fingerprint drift -> spurious attestation staleness.

## Follow-ups opened (linked discovered_from 3156, epic NOT reopened)
- BUG edfe-d8cd-5f19-46f2 — flaky graph perf tests (absolute wall-clock limits false-fail on loaded
  CI runners). Remediation options: relative/scaling metric, retry+headroom, or non-gating perf job.
- TASK 6a9a-910a-6938-4695 — editor round-trip for NET-NEW project criteria is broken: a
  project.<name> criterion can't be authored because create_prompt/_valid_id forbids '.' (and '.'
  collides with the <id>.<variant>.md overlay convention). DESIGN (grounded in Semgrep/npm/Python:
  decouple logical id from physical filename): a single canonical criterion_prompt_id(cid) maps
  project.<name> -> plan-review-project-<name> (filesystem-safe), used by BOTH descriptor resolution
  and editor authoring; project names constrained to [A-Za-z0-9-] (one namespace dot); no back-compat
  (just-shipped feature — fixtures rewritten freely). In plan-review now.

## Key files (on main)
src/rebar/llm/criteria/{model.py,overlay.py} (shared layer); plan_review/{registry.py,orchestrator.py,
det_invariants.py,workflow_ops.py}; code_review/{registry.py,detectors.py,criteria_routing.json};
workflow/criterion_preview.py + editor.py + editor_assets/src/rebarProvider.jsx; eval.py + eval_solver.py
+ _cli/_llm_commands.py; attest.py; docs/adr/0015,0016,0017; CLAUDE.md.
